features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported Q1 2021 worldwide sales of $22.3 billion, up 7.9% year-over-year, with operational growth of 5.5%. Adjusted diluted EPS was $2.59, representing a 12.6% increase. The pharmaceutical segment grew 7.1% driven by strong performance from STELARA, TREMFYA, and DARZALEX. Medical devices grew 8% as markets recovered from COVID-19 impacts, while consumer health declined 3.3% due to difficult prior-year comparisons from pandemic-related demand surges.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year 2021 adjusted operational sales growth guidance to 8.7%-9.9% and tightened adjusted EPS guidance to $9.30-$9.45. They expect pharmaceutical segment to deliver a 10th consecutive year of above-market growth driven by volume rather than price. Medical devices recovery is expected to accelerate in Q2 due to favorable year-over-year comparisons and continued market improvement. The company remains committed to investing in R&D at elevated levels to strengthen long-term competitive positioning.
features.risks: The COVID-19 vaccine faces regulatory scrutiny due to rare blood clotting events, with US distribution paused pending CDC review. Manufacturing quality issues at the Emergent BioSolutions facility could impact vaccine supply commitments. Medical device recovery remains dependent on variable factors including patient willingness to seek care, insurance coverage, and mobility restrictions. Consumer health faces continued choppy quarterly comparisons due to prior-year pandemic-related demand patterns.
features.key_drivers: Pharmaceutical growth is driven by strong performance of key immunology products STELARA and TREMFYA, oncology portfolio strength led by DARZALEX, and volume-based expansion rather than pricing. Medical device recovery is supported by procedure volume normalization, new product launches including 21 major launches planned for 2021, and market share gains across multiple platforms. The company's diversified portfolio and continued R&D investment of 14.2% of sales positions it for sustained growth beyond current recovery dynamics.
